news.title: STAT+: FDA’s top gene therapy regulator is leaving his role
news.summary: The departure of Vijay Kumar follows a broader leadership shakeup at the FDA.

news.title: STAT+: AstraZeneca to pay $34 million to settle kickback charges filed by Texas attorney general
news.summary: AstraZeneca agreed to pay $34 million to settle claims that the company paid kickbacks to improperly influence prescriptions paid for by Texas Medicaid.

news.title: STAT+: 26 states sue to block Medicaid work requirements
news.summary: States argue the rules will make it too hard for people to get the Medicaid coverage they're entitled too.

news.title: 5 takeaways from STAT’s investigation into microhospital operator Nutex Health
news.summary: Read the takeaways from STAT's investigation into Nutex, which used the No Surprises Act to fuel a stunning financial turnaround.

news.title: STAT+: Unredacted comments from California hospital lawsuits show stunning admissions and a ‘forced’ McKinsey collaboration
news.summary: Who controls the Medicaid market, GLP-1 usage and pizza household costs, and more from Health Care Inc.

news.title: STAT+: Longevity, wellness physicians named to panel advising FDA on peptides
news.summary: The panel will soon consider whether to relax restrictions on certain peptides.

news.title: STAT+: Talawar bets bispecific approach can shake up eczema market
news.summary: Moderna still bullish on mRNA, Ipsen's acquisition of Kartos, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a broader $35 insulin cap, a former FDA cancer drug chief, and more
news.summary: The search for affordability wins is leading U.S. senators back to capping the cost of insulin at $35 in the private insurance market and for the uninsured

news.title: What’s next for 988 LGBTQ+ services?
news.summary: Private ERs, pulmonary hypertension, and other health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Moderna co-founder Kenneth Chien on the future of mRNA — and Moderna
news.summary: In a Q&A, Kenneth Chien, no longer with Moderna, predicts the company's mRNA cancer vaccine will be transformative for biotech.

news.title: STAT+: Investor Clive Meanwell on designing biotechs for population health, and AI’s role as a catalyst
news.summary: Clive Meanwell, chairman of investment firm Population Health Partners, was an AI skeptic until recently. Now, he thinks it's a catalyst.

news.title: STAT+: Sword Health contracted to provide AI-supported physical therapy for an entire country
news.summary: Portugal's National Health Service signed a deal with digital health company Sword for its AI-assisted virtual physical therapy care.

news.title: Opinion: STAT+: The U.S.-China biotech crackdown may hurt the scientists America needs the most
news.summary: “There is a difference between a policy based on evidence and a broad legislative response that targets China-origin entities as inherently suspicious,” writes Brian Yang.

news.title: Opinion: Banning gender-affirming care doesn’t protect children — it makes it harder to help them
news.summary: “Physician groups betraying the public’s trust out of fear of government retaliation has permanent consequences,” writes a plastic surgeon.

news.title: STAT+: The ERs that can turn patients away — and are reaping millions
news.summary: The hospital operator Nutex enriched itself by exploiting loopholes in laws meant to protect patients, STAT found.

news.title: How payers, drugmakers can collaborate to drive more outcomes-based contracts
news.summary: LAS VEGAS — As drug costs and spending continue to rise, the demand for solutions opens the door to closer collaboration between payers and manufacturers.

news.title: HRSA opens applications for $140M in rural health grant funding
news.summary: Substance use disorder, medical residencies, rural integrated networks and telehealth are among the administration's targets for the funds.

news.title: HHS announces new oversight measures for TEFCA, touts 1B health records exchanged
news.summary: ONC unveiled new efforts to strengthen oversight of TEFCA, including hiring a federal IT contractor to conduct audits and reviews of TEFCA participants.

news.title: ACA marketplace enrollment down by 3M as of February, new federal data show
news.summary: New federal data show that 19.2 million individuals were enrolled in Affordable Care Act marketplace plans as of February, down by nearly 3 million from 2025.

news.title: Clinic network CEO pleads guilty to embezzling millions for 'social media influenced' market trading
news.summary: Patrick Alan Bucknum's "poor trades" led him to transfer more and more funds away from Washington's Community Clinic Network to try and recover his losses, according to the Department of Justice.

news.title: Fierce Healthcare Fundraising Tracker '26: Trase secures $107M to scale AI agents; xCures lands $46M
news.summary: Keep up with all the venture capital dollars being funneled into the health tech and digital health industries.

news.title: Doctronic, Simple HealthKit partner to connect at-home screening with AI-powered clinical care
news.summary: AI startup Doctronic is partnering with Simple HealthKit, an at-home health screening platform, to offer consumers end-to-end diagnostic screening integrated with virtual clinical care.

news.title: One Brooklyn Health Selects hellocare.ai to Advance AI-Powered Virtual Care Across Its Hospitals
news.summary: This partnership reinforces OBH’s commitment to innovation and positions the organization at the forefront of AI-driven healthcare transformation, delivering safer, smarter, and more efficient care to the communities it serves.

news.title: 3 in 10 adults turn to AI or social media for health advice, citing difficulties accessing and affording care
news.summary: A recent KFF poll found that roughly three in ten adults self-reported turning to social media (31%) or AI chatbots (29%) at least monthly for health information and advice.

news.title: As PBM industry shifts, LucyRx and Abarca Health merge to build scale
news.summary: Amid significant shifts in the pharmacy benefit management industry, LucyRx and Abarca Health have revealed plans to merge to build the scale necessary to compete in this changing landscape.

news.title: Clinicians' compensation rose 4.3% across 2025: AMGA
news.summary: The annual survey of 451 medical groups and health systems found signs of "genuine demand expansion" and productivity increases that support about half of the year's compensation growth.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Trump taps Klomp as HHS Deputy Secretary; Rush, CHOP CEOs to retire
news.summary: Welcome to this week's Chutes & Ladders, our roundup of hirings, firings and retirings throughout the industry. Please submit the good news—or the bad—from your shop, and we will feature it here at the end of each week.

news.title: FDA rejects Sobi’s gout drug over manufacturing issues, sparing Amgen blockbuster
news.summary: Sobi’s plan to introduce a challenger to Amgen’s blockbuster gout drug Krystexxa has been put on hold by the FDA.

news.title: BioCryst ends internal discovery programs, closes Alabama facility in pivot to cost-saving partnerships
news.summary: After scrapping development on its diabetic macular edema treatment avoralstat last month, BioCryst has announced it is shuttering all internal discovery programs to focus on external innovation and partnerships.

news.title: Evommune’s phase 2b trial flops, ending challenge to Novartis in hives
news.summary: A phase 2b trial of Evommune’s EVO756 has missed its primary endpoint, wiping out a would-be rival to Novartis for the chronic spontaneous urticaria market.

news.title: United Therapeutics nabs FDA approval for donor-lung assessment device
news.summary: United Therapeutics has been given a premarket approval from the FDA for its LungFX device.

news.title: Roche launches Axelios 1, its eagerly awaited gene sequencing rival to Illumina
news.summary: Roche may have failed to buy out Illumina back in 2012, but now it’s trying to take up some of the gene sequencing specialist’s market share with its own next-gen platform.

news.title: Production problems hamper Lantheus bid for FDA approval of radiopharma dx kit
news.summary: The FDA dealt Lantheus’ bid to garner approval of its radiodiopharmaceutical diagnostic kit a setback due to manufacturing issues at a third-party facility.

news.title: Ipsen pens $1.7B deal to acquire Kartos for potential Jakafi add-on therapy for myelofibrosis
news.summary: Ipsen is paying $450 million upfront to buy U.S.-based Kartos Therapeutics for a phase 3-stage blood cancer drug designed to improve Jakafi treatment.

news.title: UK-based Scancell bids to buy Nasdaq-listed Neuphoria to ‘increase profile’ with US investors
news.summary: As Neuphoria continues to navigate the fallout of its failed social anxiety program, the biotech is mulling an offer from another potential buyer.

news.title: 'There's room for multiple winners': Talawar heads to Nasdaq via SPAC deal to bankroll eczema asset
news.summary: Amid a flurry of traditional IPOs this spring, Talawar Therapeutics is taking a different path to the public markets via a merger with a special purpose acquisition company.

news.title: AstraZeneca unveils phase 3 bone disease data behind $5B forecast for Strensiq successor
news.summary: AstraZeneca has shared the data behind previously disclosed phase 3 rare disease wins, fleshing out the case for a Strensiq successor tipped to generate blockbuster sales. The company has yet to share data from a third trial that missed its primary endpoint.

news.title: Cash-strapped Alopexx reignites plans to go public via $30M IPO to fund infectious disease programs
news.summary: Alopexx has resuscitated its plans for a public listing as the biotech seeks urgently needed funds to advance a pipeline that includes an infectious disease antibody that was once the focus of a $375 million deal with Sanofi.

news.title: Epicrispr gene silencer is 1st drug to boost muscle in form of muscular dystrophy, biotech says
news.summary: Epicrispr Biotechnologies has used its gene silencing tech to boost muscle mass in three patients with facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy, a clinical first for the debilitating disease with no approved treatments.

news.title: WAIC 2026 × 36氪「氪话未来直播间」：带着AI去前线
news.summary: 2026WAIC举办，36氪将设「氪话未来直播间」

papers.title: One Forward Beats Two: InnerZoom for Accurate and Efficient GUI Grounding
papers.summary: MLLM-based GUI grounding methods commonly formulate target localization as autoregressive coordinate generation, enabling models to leverage the strong instruction-following and semantic understanding capabilities of MLLMs. However, this formulation requires the model to retain region-level target evidence while decoding coordinate tokens with the spatial precision demanded by GUI clicking. Our diagnostic analysis reveals that target-region awareness emerges in intermediate decoder layers but is neither retained nor translated into the final coordinate prediction. Existing ZoomIn-style methods

papers.title: ReasoningLens: Hierarchical Visualization and Diagnostic Auditing for Large Reasoning Models
papers.summary: The emergence of Large Reasoning Models has introduced exceptionally long Chain-of-Thought traces, creating a transparency burden where critical logic is often buried under massive procedural text. To address this, we present ReasoningLens, an open-source framework designed for the hierarchical visualization and diagnostic auditing of complex reasoning chains. ReasoningLens addresses information necropsy by: (1) structuring traces into interactive hierarchies that separate high-level strategy from low-level execution; (2) leveraging an agentic auditor for automated error detection and tool-aug

papers.title: The Galaxy's Guide to the Tokenizer: A Benchmark for Scientific Foundation Models
papers.summary: Tokenization is central to adapting scientific data for transformer-based foundation models, yet its impact on learned representations remains poorly understood. We compare four tokenization strategies, Affine, AIM, JetFormer, and VQ-VAE, within a unified transformer framework for astronomical imaging. Using 640,000 galaxy images from the DESI Legacy Survey and a shared AstroPT backbone, we evaluate each method on reconstruction fidelity and prediction of physical properties. Our results reveal trade-offs across approaches. The flow-based JetFormer achieves higher reconstruction quality, while

papers.title: MemoBench: Benchmarking World Modeling in Dynamically Changing Environments
papers.summary: Video generation models aspire to simulate dynamic environments, and several benchmarks now evaluate memory consistency across frames. However, most assess consistency only while the target remains in view, and the few that force objects out of view evaluate static scenes where nothing changes during occlusion. To bridge this gap, we introduce MemoBench, a diagnostic benchmark built around the disappear-and-reappear paradigm in dynamically changing environments: a target object undergoes a physical process, disappears from view, and must be correctly recovered in its updated state upon reappea

papers.title: SingGuard: A Policy-Adaptive Multimodal LLM Guardrail with Dynamic Reasoning
papers.summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed in consumer, medical, financial, and enterprise applications. This broad deployment expands the safety surface: risks can arise from multimodal question answering, assistant responses, and cross-modal composition, while moderation policies may vary across products, regions, and deployment stages. Most existing guardrails either rely on fixed taxonomies or target only a narrow set of interaction settings, which limits their adaptability when safety rules change at deployment time. We present SingGuard, a policy-adaptive multimodal guardrai
